# The aswritten perspective

> The machine-readable public perspective of aswritten.ai: an organizational
> knowledge graph of discourse acts — who said what, when, and what they made
> of it — materialized from the company's own use of its product.

Live install for MCP-capable agents: call aswritten_perspective with id "customer-architecture-demo-enterprise-c1cb" against https://aswritten.ai's MCP endpoint — see https://docs.aswritten.ai for setup.

## Areas
- [ActorIncentiveAnalysis](hub-ActorIncentiveAnalysis.html): This hub maps the actors around the product and what each one wants. The buyer question is answered concretely in the VC
- [Architecture](hub-Architecture.html): The Architecture hub frames an organization's worldview as the single source of truth from which everything else is rend
- [ArchitectureOverview](hub-ArchitectureOverview.html): The architecture has evolved from a directory-based compilation model toward a transform-based perspective engine, prior
- [AudienceFrames](hub-AudienceFrames.html): This hub gathers how the pitch and fundraising materials get reframed per audience. The structural spine is that every s
- [Behaviors](hub-Behaviors.html): This hub gathers the system behaviors of a git-native memory product — both the low-level operations and the user-facing
- [CallToAction](hub-CallToAction.html): This hub gathers how the fundraise should *land* with prospective investors — the concrete ask and the path to becoming 
- [CaseStudies](hub-CaseStudies.html): This hub gathers case studies meant to demonstrate worldview compilation in action. Three are framed as direct compariso
- [ChangeManagement](hub-ChangeManagement.html): This hub gathers how change flows through aswritten's repo-native operations — across handoffs, research promotion, revi
- [CompetitiveLandscape](hub-CompetitiveLandscape.html): This hub positions aswritten against everyone adjacent by a single axis: rivals do orchestration or retrieval of what's 
- [CompliancePosture](hub-CompliancePosture.html): This hub gathers how aswritten frames compliance — as both a revenue lever and an adoption gate. The mainline reads comp
- [Conviction](hub-Conviction.html): This hub gathers the conviction construct: the epistemic settledness of organizational knowledge, formalized as a Notion
- [CoreNarratives](hub-CoreNarratives.html): This hub tracks the evolution of aswritten.ai’s competitive positioning, anchored by the thesis that the next bottleneck
- [CoreWorkflows](hub-CoreWorkflows.html): This hub gathers the operational patterns aswritten runs on. The flagship is the session-to-document arc: Interview → Sa
- [DataIntegrationFlows](hub-DataIntegrationFlows.html): This hub gathers the plumbing by which raw activity becomes queryable knowledge. On the execution side, the webhook-mode
- [DataModelLifecycle](hub-DataModelLifecycle.html): This hub gathers the architectural design of a data-model lifecycle built on Git. The spine is an append-only, Git-centr
- [DesignTradeoffs](hub-DesignTradeoffs.html): This hub gathers the recurring design tensions in the system's architecture and vocabulary. A foundational pair is frame
- [Differentiators](hub-Differentiators.html): This hub tracks the evolving definition of aswritten’s competitive moat, moving from feature-based differentiators to a 
- [EnablementTraining](hub-EnablementTraining.html): This hub gathers the enablement/onboarding thinking for aswritten's beta. The framing is top-down: CTO the customer's CT
- [ExpansionPathway](hub-ExpansionPathway.html): This hub gathers how aswritten grows from a common entry — a [[amount] pilot] — into divergent tracks: Core for Product/
- [FeedbackLoops](hub-FeedbackLoops.html): This hub gathers the mechanics of how feedback enters and reshapes the product and its knowledge. Two loops sit at the c
- [Flows](hub-Flows.html): This hub gathers the process flows of the system — how knowledge gets in, how it gets out, and how the loop closes. On c
- [FocusSequencing](hub-FocusSequencing.html): This hub gathers competing go-to-market sequencing theses — how to enter, what to prove first, and when to expand. One m
- [FrictionFailure](hub-FrictionFailure.html): This hub tracks the friction points in AI adoption, development, and sales. Development friction is dominated by AI code
- [GovernanceSafetyTrust](hub-GovernanceSafetyTrust.html): This hub gathers two distinct registers of governance, safety, and trust. The conceptual register frames AI through AI i
- [HiringPlan](hub-HiringPlan.html): This hub holds the company's staffing arc, rooted in a solo-founder capacity ceiling — \"Solo founder maxes out at ~1.5 
- [InflectionPoints](hub-InflectionPoints.html): A mixed hub gathering the moments Scarlet flagged as inflections — strategic, market, and tactical. The dominant frame i
- [IntakePrioritization](hub-IntakePrioritization.html): This hub gathers the intake-and-prioritization mechanics of a content-production workflow. The spine is a first-class In
- [KPIsSuccess](hub-KPIsSuccess.html): This hub gathers aswritten's success metrics across two horizons. The long-horizon KPI is an acquisition-sized exit Scar
- [LeadingIndicators](hub-LeadingIndicators.html): This hub gathers the leading-indicator signals tracked from early customer use. The dominant thesis (Jan 17): the cite +
- [MarketContext](hub-MarketContext.html): This hub gathers the framings of the market problem the venture addresses, all circling a single root — AI tooling starv
- [Milestones](hub-Milestones.html): This hub gathers product and partnership milestones across spring 2026. The mainline is aswritten's own launch: a beta a
- [Mission](hub-Mission.html): The Mission hub centers on building collective memory as infrastructure — explicitly \"infrastructure, not tool\" — that
- [MoatLeverage](hub-MoatLeverage.html): This hub gathers the claim that AsWritten's defensibility and its reach are the same asset: a Git-native narrative archi
- [ModulesCapabilities](hub-ModulesCapabilities.html): This hub gathers the product's tool surface and capability decisions, mostly dated March–May 2026. The dominant arc is a
- [Narrative_1](hub-Narrative_1.html): This hub holds the thesis that identity follows narrative architecture — that steering a narrative shapes what an AI bec
- [Narrative_AIContextValidation](hub-Narrative_AIContextValidation.html): This hub gathers the case for AI context validation as a distinct value proposition. The throughline: Aswritten as conte
- [Narrative_B2CPivot](hub-Narrative_B2CPivot.html): This hub holds aswritten's reframing of its primary market. On Mar 17, Scarlet and Tony independently converged on B2C o
- [Narrative_BoundedDomainFidelityArgument](hub-Narrative_BoundedDomainFidelityArgument.html): Argues that expertise modules are higher fidelity than org memory because courses, books, and consulting deliverables ar
- [Narrative_CategoryCuratedExpertiseInstallation](hub-Narrative_CategoryCuratedExpertiseInstallation.html): The category began as 'curated expertise installation' (Strategy Doc V1), then narrowed: in Mar Scarlet dropped 'curated
- [Narrative_ClojureOwnsLogic](hub-Narrative_ClojureOwnsLogic.html): This hub argues the orchestration logic belongs in Clojure, with the build-vs-wrap boundary drawn explicitly. The event-
- [Narrative_ContinuousCompliance](hub-Narrative_ContinuousCompliance.html): This hub frames a move from snapshot-based to continuous compliance. The mainline diagnoses the problem — point-in-time 
- [Narrative_EnterpriseShapePrimaryFocus](hub-Narrative_EnterpriseShapePrimaryFocus.html): On 2026-04-21 Scarlet applied Tony's additive-vs-incremental test to her funnel and tentatively named the the enterprise

- [Narrative_OrgWorldview](hub-Narrative_OrgWorldview.html): This hub gathers the org's account of how aswritten builds its understanding of a client. Two foundation-level children 
- [Narrative_PerspectiveOnSpec](hub-Narrative_PerspectiveOnSpec.html): This hub gathers the April thesis that a perspective built *on spec* — before any prospect interview — is itself the pit
- [Narrative_PerspectiveVsInformation](hub-Narrative_PerspectiveVsInformation.html): This hub argues the load-bearing distinction for modules versus RAG: modules deliver *perspective*, not information. The
- [Narrative_SalesAsDelivery](hub-Narrative_SalesAsDelivery.html): This hub holds the thesis that the sales motion and product delivery are one thing — Demo 1 *is* the sale, and every cal
- [Narrative_SeparationPrincipleProductVsOrg](hub-Narrative_SeparationPrincipleProductVsOrg.html): This hub gathers a single dated demolition pass (Mar 2026) enacting one principle: collapse the product/org split by eli
- [Narrative_ThreePartSeedingPattern](hub-Narrative_ThreePartSeedingPattern.html): A three-step onboarding playbook for seeding aswritten.ai from historical recordings, articulated around the enterprise 
- [Narrative_TranscriptBootstrappingUnderplayed](hub-Narrative_TranscriptBootstrappingUnderplayed.html): This hub argues transcript bootstrapping is aswritten's strongest underused onramp — and that the company keeps failing 
- [Narrative_UserFacingVsInternalLanguageSplit](hub-Narrative_UserFacingVsInternalLanguageSplit.html): This hub holds the deliberate two-layer language split: human words on the user-facing surface, engineering metaphor und
- [NarrativeAnchor](hub-NarrativeAnchor.html): This hub gathers the meanings of *narrative* at the center of aswritten.ai. The substantive core casts narrative as the 
- [NarrativeDrivenRoadmap](hub-NarrativeDrivenRoadmap.html): This roadmap hub braids two strands. The go-to-market strand reframes positioning from consulting-first toward a \"train
- [NorthStarMetrics](hub-NorthStarMetrics.html): This hub gathers the candidate \"north star\" signals — and they do not agree on one number. The strongest product claim
- [OperatingModelShifts](hub-OperatingModelShifts.html): The hub traces a multi-front shift in how work, staffing, and AI systems are organized. In Jul 2025, Scarlet and Tony fr
- [Opportunity](hub-Opportunity.html): The opportunity is framed through three distinct problem lenses, none crowned. The strategy-execution disconnect casts i
- [OrganizationalChangeManual](hub-OrganizationalChangeManual.html): This hub gathers field notes toward a manual for organizational change in AI adoption — the recurring claim being that t
- [Outcomes](hub-Outcomes.html): This hub gathers the claimed outcomes of the product, dominated by a quantified ROI stack for a 10-person team: onboardi
- [OutcomesProof](hub-OutcomesProof.html): OutcomesProof gathers heterogeneous evidence that the system delivers value. Commercially, the live-demo delivery gate t

- [PackagingPricing](hub-PackagingPricing.html): Pricing strategy has moved from flat per-repo models toward a multi-tier structure that distinguishes between self-serve
- [PersonasJTBD](hub-PersonasJTBD.html): This hub holds aswritten's persona/JTBD model, which has migrated over time. An original four-archetype scheme — Knowled
- [PositioningThesis](hub-PositioningThesis.html): This hub gathers how aswritten.ai positions itself, narrating an evolution rather than a settled label. The mainline ref
- [PrimaryActors](hub-PrimaryActors.html): This hub gathers the cast of who the product is *for* and *with* — and the children do not agree on a single primary act
- [Primitives](hub-Primitives.html): This hub gathers the write primitives and structural definitions underlying the aswritten knowledge graph. The keystone 
- [Process](hub-Process.html): A June 12 process-redesign session whose stated aim was \"I would like to improve the mess\", structured as correcting f
- [ProcessPolicyUpdates](hub-ProcessPolicyUpdates.html): This hub collects process and policy decisions across the pipeline, CI, branching, and protocol placement. The earliest 
- [Product](hub-Product.html): This hub gathers Scarlet's product lineage and the through-line connecting it: extracting narrative from data to steer o
- [ProductLadder](hub-ProductLadder.html): This hub gathers the product-and-go-to-market ladder. The commercial spine is the four-phase core expansion track: [a [a
- [ProductOverview](hub-ProductOverview.html): The ProductOverview hub gathers the shape of the aswritten product across UX, architecture, packaging, pricing, and tool
- [Proof](hub-Proof.html): The proof hub tracks how aswritten demonstrates that it changes AI behavior — across sales pitch, technical verification
- [QualityReviewGates](hub-QualityReviewGates.html): This hub spans the review, evaluation, and verification disciplines that gate knowledge quality. The review protocol clu
- [Quotes](hub-Quotes.html): This hub is the verbatim record of the aswritten narrative — direct speech and attributed observations spanning January 
- [RiskGuardrails](hub-RiskGuardrails.html): This hub gathers the risks and guardrails threaded through Aswritten's company narrative — product, sales, hiring, capit
- [RoleTopology](hub-RoleTopology.html): This hub gathers how aswritten models *who occupies what* in an organization. The mainline distinction is firm and dated
- [SalesDecks](hub-SalesDecks.html): This hub tracks the evolution of sales-as-delivery, where the act of selling is indistinguishable from the product itsel
- [ScalabilityPerformance](hub-ScalabilityPerformance.html): This hub gathers the scalability and performance work, dominated by a dated infra trajectory. In Mar (26th), memory reba
- [SecurityPrivacyModel](hub-SecurityPrivacyModel.html): The hub gathers how secrets, identity, and compliance are handled. The spine is credential hygiene: the target credentia
- [Segmentation](hub-Segmentation.html): This hub gathers competing cuts at *who the customer is*. The quantitative spine is a dev-shop ICP — [5-150 people, [amo
- [Signals](hub-Signals.html): A mixed gather of market, product, and self-observation signals — no single arc, several distinct registers. The stronge
- [SolutionArchetypes](hub-SolutionArchetypes.html): This hub gathers the solution archetypes and target use cases the product addresses, mostly via `skos:broader`. The unif
- [StackOverview](hub-StackOverview.html): This hub gathers the As Written / Scarlet Dame technical-and-conceptual stack across two registers. The conceptual mainl
- [Strategy](hub-Strategy.html): This hub gathers the strategic thesis that narrative is an organizational operating system — a framework embedding narra
- [StrategyOverview](hub-StrategyOverview.html): This hub gathers the strategy behind the strategic rebrand from storyBASE/witness to aswritten.ai, plus the foundational
- [SystemTopology](hub-SystemTopology.html): This hub gathers how aswritten's system is structured across infrastructure, tooling, and perspective. The mainline is a
- [TechnicalExplainers](hub-TechnicalExplainers.html): This hub gathers material on how technical concepts get *explained* — both a design decision and the deck/communication 
- [TechnologiesSocialSystems](hub-TechnologiesSocialSystems.html): This hub gathers the technical and conceptual scaffolding behind treating organizations as steerable systems. The intell
- [TimingThesis](hub-TimingThesis.html): This hub gathers the \"why now\" arguments for the venture, running along two distinct rails. The first is IP leakage as
- [ValueExchange](hub-ValueExchange.html): This hub gathers how value is exchanged and articulated around the collective-memory product. The pivotal moment is a re
- [Vision](hub-Vision.html): Scarlet's Vision holds a steering-vector thesis as its through-line: language, narrative, and meaning act as steering ve
- [WhatDoesItDo](hub-WhatDoesItDo.html): This hub answers \"what does it do\" in two registers that pull in different directions. The product spine: As Written i
- [WhatIsIt](hub-WhatIsIt.html): This hub answers \"what is the product?\" and its mainline, dated April 28 2026, is positive-identity: the product is cu
- [WhatItsNot](hub-WhatItsNot.html): This hub collects the positioning negatives — what aswritten is *not*. The load-bearing boundary is search vs. aswritten

## Voice & style
- [AICompanyizationCase](hub-AICompanyizationCase.html): This hub gathers observations about capitalization and typographic emphasis as authorial style signals. The dominant pat
- [Analogy](hub-Analogy.html): This hub gathers the analogies and metaphors the narrative reaches for to make AI-collective-memory legible, spanning se
- [Anaphora](hub-Anaphora.html): This hub gathers style observations of anaphora — repeated clause-initial structure used for emphasis, rhythm, and memor
- [Antithesis](hub-Antithesis.html): This hub gathers uses of antithesis as a recurring rhetorical pattern across the aswritten.ai narrative and pitch. Most 
- [BrandNameStylization](hub-BrandNameStylization.html): This hub gathers observations about orthographic and brand stylization across aswritten's documents. The dominant patter
- [CadenceRhythm](hub-CadenceRhythm.html): This hub gathers the document's cadence and rhythm devices. The dominant engine is the rule of three: a git-native triad
- [Calibration](hub-Calibration.html): Calibration gathers how aswritten validates its own quality — the recurring frame is optometric. The optometrist model a
- [CitationConventions](hub-CitationConventions.html): This hub collects observations about how spoken and written evidence is cited across the perspective's documents. The do
- [FlowCoherence](hub-FlowCoherence.html): This hub gathers style observations of a single first-person analytical document, narrating its voice and architecture r
- [GrammarSyntax](hub-GrammarSyntax.html): Grammar and syntax observations of Scarlet's writing converge on a few load-bearing habits. Voice is overwhelmingly acti
- [IdiolectPhrasing](hub-IdiolectPhrasing.html): This hub gathers idiolect and register observations across speakers. Tony's voice dominates the enthusiasm register: mil
- [InformationArchitecture](hub-InformationArchitecture.html): This hub gathers Scarlet/aswritten style and information-architecture observations — how the documents physically encode
- [JargonPolicy](hub-JargonPolicy.html): This hub tracks the evolution of jargon policy, oscillating between internal-audience density and explicit terminology c
- [KeyPhrases](hub-KeyPhrases.html): This hub catalogs the core lexicon and rhetorical architecture defining the collective memory pitch. The discourse is an
- [Metaphor](hub-Metaphor.html): This hub gathers the document's figurative language across two registers. Empowerment/control metaphors dominate: narrat
- [NamingConventions](hub-NamingConventions.html): This hub gathers the project's naming and positioning conventions, which cohere around two devices. First, a consistent 
- [NarrativePillars](hub-NarrativePillars.html): This hub gathers two unlike strands under one roof. The bulk is a cluster of style observations on how strategic prose i
- [Parallelism](hub-Parallelism.html): This hub gathers observations of parallelism as a recurring stylistic device across an AI-native strategy/pitch document
- [PlainLanguage](hub-PlainLanguage.html): This hub gathers observations of Scarlet's plain-language style across founder-facing documents. The dominant thread is 
- [ProblemStakes](hub-ProblemStakes.html): This hub frames the stakes of the problem the pitch addresses, and across its children one rhetorical move dominates: op
- [PunctuationTypography](hub-PunctuationTypography.html): This hub catalogs the typographic and punctuation conventions defining the project's prose architecture. The em-dash has
- [RegisterFormality](hub-RegisterFormality.html): This hub collects observations about register and formality across the corpus. The dominant pattern is mixed register: s
- [ResonanceUse](hub-ResonanceUse.html): This hub gathers how *resonant framing* gets used — the rhetorical and structural devices that make dense thinking land.
- [RhetoricalDevices](hub-RhetoricalDevices.html): This hub gathers observed rhetorical devices in the source document, spanning structure, contrast, metaphor, and repetit
- [RhetoricalQuestion](hub-RhetoricalQuestion.html): This hub collects style observations on the rhetorical question as a recurring device across the org's writing. The domi
- [Rubric_Accuracy](hub-Rubric_Accuracy.html): This hub gathers accuracy assessments across the corpus's document types — memos, pitch decks, ADRs, call notes, and col
- [Rubric_Cadence](hub-Rubric_Cadence.html): This hub gathers per-document cadence assessments under one rubric dimension — how rhythm, sentence-length variation, an
- [Rubric_Flow](hub-Rubric_Flow.html): This hub gathers rubric assessments of *flow* — logical progression, transitions, and structural coherence — across a wi
- [Rubric_Novelty](hub-Rubric_Novelty.html): This hub gathers per-artifact novelty assessments scored under one rubric — each child judges how original a given docum
- [Rubric_Phrasing](hub-Rubric_Phrasing.html): This hub gathers per-document/per-transcript phrasing assessments from a writing rubric. The dominant finding repeats ac
- [Rubric_Register](hub-Rubric_Register.html): This hub collects per-document register assessments under one rubric dimension across the corpus. The dominant reading i
- [Rubric_Resonance](hub-Rubric_Resonance.html): This hub collects rubric assessments of resonance — how well each document, claim, or rebrand lands emotionally and memo
- [Rubric_StrategicAlignment](hub-Rubric_StrategicAlignment.html): This hub gathers strategic-alignment rubric assessments across the corpus, and the dominant reading is striking uniformi
- [Rubric_Tailoring](hub-Rubric_Tailoring.html): This hub gathers per-document audience-tailoring assessments scored against the rubric. The dominant axis is *who the wr
- [RuleOfThree](hub-RuleOfThree.html): This hub gathers observations of the document's recurring rule-of-three rhetoric — triadic parallelism used for emphasis
- [SecondPerson](hub-SecondPerson.html): This hub gathers style observations on second-person and direct address across aswritten's documents. The mainline is a 
- [ShortPunchyCadence](hub-ShortPunchyCadence.html): This hub gathers style observations on a recurring short, punchy cadence across the founder's speech and writing. The do
- [Simile](hub-Simile.html): This hub collects the figurative devices the speaker reaches for — predominantly similes and conceptual metaphors drawn 
- [SpeakerIdiolects](hub-SpeakerIdiolects.html): This hub collects observations about how individual speakers sound — their idiolects, register markers, and the editoria
- [StockPhrases](hub-StockPhrases.html): This hub gathers recurring stock phrases observed in the speaker's style, spanning several registers. The dominant ancho
- [StyleMetrics](hub-StyleMetrics.html): This hub gathers per-document style and readability metrics across the org's corpus, computed on the same axes: active-v
- [Tagline](hub-Tagline.html): This hub gathers the candidate taglines and brand-voice observations for aswritten.ai, whose name puns Latin *sic* (as w
- [TerminologyControl](hub-TerminologyControl.html): This hub gathers the project's controlled vocabulary — the terms AsWritten/a collaborator insists on and the stylization
- [ToneAuthoritative](hub-ToneAuthoritative.html): This hub tracks the authoritative register defining aswritten’s internal documentation. The style is characterized by ac
- [ToneDirectPersonal](hub-ToneDirectPersonal.html): This hub gathers stylistic observations about a direct, second-person, personal voice running through the product's mark
- [ToneVoice](hub-ToneVoice.html): This hub gathers observations of a single brand voice across registers. The dominant mode is confident and agent-centere
- [VerbChoice](hub-VerbChoice.html): This hub gathers style observations on verb choice across aswritten.ai's pitch and messaging. The dominant pattern is a 
- [VoiceActive](hub-VoiceActive.html): This hub gathers style observations of an active, agentive voice across the company's writing. The dominant pattern is f

## Reference
- [Entity index](entities.html): every published node with attribution
- [aswritten.ai](https://aswritten.ai): the product
- [docs.aswritten.ai](https://docs.aswritten.ai): protocol and setup
